summary: Do not ship: S-1 remains open because the canonical freeze can stay green while Terraform removes or disables the ruleset.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Canonical extraction ignores the HCL context that determines whether the ruleset exists
findings.body: The guard extracts only from the textual resource header through the next column-zero `}`, so context outside that range is invisible. Wrapping the unchanged ruleset block with `/*` and `*/` produced `commented_resource_equals=yes` while `terraform fmt -check -` still parsed successfully; Terraform then sees no ruleset. A second bypass is a tracked `*_override.tf` containing the same resource address with `count = 0` or `enforcement = "disabled"`: Terraform merges override files after normal files while both guarded files remain byte-identical ([HashiCorp override semantics](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/files/override)). Thus Seam A and all current witnesses can remain green with F-0 absent or disabled.
findings.recommendation: Replace range-based text extraction with an HCL-aware, module-wide structural check that proves the blocks are active top-level definitions and rejects tracked override files for these addresses. Add mutation witnesses for an enclosing multiline comment and a `*_override.tf` that sets `count = 0` or disables enforcement.

next_steps: Keep S-1 open until both module-context bypasses fail the required gate.
